meta.name: Awesome LLM Reasoning Roadmap
meta.subtitle: From Chain-of-Thought to Test-Time Scaling and Reasoning RL
meta.scope: Focused roadmap for LLM reasoning papers, methods, code links, and unofficial reproduction entries.

papers.title: Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems
papers.takeaway: Early demonstration that verifier-guided selection can improve mathematical reasoning outputs.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.takeaway: The canonical paper that made intermediate natural-language reasoning traces a standard interface.

papers.title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
papers.takeaway: Shows that sampling multiple reasoning paths and marginalizing answers can beat greedy CoT.

papers.title: STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning
papers.takeaway: Bootstraps rationales from the model itself, foreshadowing self-improvement loops for reasoning.

papers.title: Least-to-Most Prompting Enables Complex Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.takeaway: Turns hard reasoning into a sequence of easier subproblems, making decomposition an explicit strategy.

papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
papers.takeaway: Connects reasoning traces with actions, observations, and tool-like environments.

papers.title: Program-Aided Language Models
papers.takeaway: Uses programs as executable reasoning traces, separating symbolic execution from language generation.

papers.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
papers.takeaway: Self-supervises tool-use decisions, a precursor to tool-augmented reasoning systems.

papers.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
papers.takeaway: Adds verbal feedback and episodic memory to language agents for iterative improvement.

papers.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
papers.takeaway: Frames reasoning improvement as generate-feedback-revise without external labels.

papers.title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
papers.takeaway: Turns reasoning into explicit search over intermediate thought states.

papers.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
papers.takeaway: Popularized process supervision for mathematical reasoning, not just final-answer supervision.

papers.title: Graph of Thoughts: Solving Elaborate Problems with Large Language Models
papers.takeaway: Generalizes thought structures from chains and trees to graph-like transformations.

papers.title: ToRA: A Tool-Integrated Reasoning Agent for Mathematical Problem Solving
papers.takeaway: Combines tool-integrated trajectories with math reasoning, bridging CoT and executable agents.

papers.title: MetaMath: Bootstrap Your Own Mathematical Questions for Large Language Models
papers.takeaway: Shows the importance of data bootstrapping and question rewriting for math reasoning.

papers.title: MATH-Shepherd: Verify and Reinforce LLMs Step-by-step without Human Annotations
papers.takeaway: Builds step-level supervision without dense human annotations, linking verification to scalable reward construction.

papers.title: Self-Rewarding Language Models
papers.takeaway: Explores using the model itself as a judge, a key motif in scalable preference and reward pipelines.

papers.title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models
papers.takeaway: Introduced GRPO in a math reasoning setting and set up much of the later DeepSeek-R1 storyline.

papers.title: Quiet-STaR: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Think Before Speaking
papers.takeaway: Extends latent/internal rationale ideas by training models to generate useful intermediate thoughts.

papers.title: Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally can be More Effective than Scaling Model Parameters
papers.takeaway: Frames inference-time compute as a scaling axis, setting the stage for budget forcing and search-like inference.

papers.title: Qwen2.5-Math Technical Report: Toward Mathematical Expert Model via Self-Improvement
papers.takeaway: Shows a modern open math model pipeline with self-improvement and math-centric training.

papers.title: DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report
papers.takeaway: A frontier MoE technical report that contextualizes the base-model side of the DeepSeek reasoning line.

papers.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.takeaway: The central public reference for RL-driven long-chain reasoning and distillation into smaller models.

papers.title: Kimi k1.5: Scaling Reinforcement Learning with LLMs
papers.takeaway: Connects long-context RL and reasoning scaling, useful for comparing with DeepSeek-R1.

papers.title: s1: Simple test-time scaling
papers.takeaway: A minimal and memorable reference for budget forcing and inference-time reasoning control.

papers.title: LIMO: Less is More for Reasoning
papers.takeaway: Argues that small but carefully selected reasoning data can unlock strong reasoning behavior.

papers.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM Reinforcement Learning System at Scale
papers.takeaway: A systems-oriented RL recipe for large-scale reasoning training.

papers.title: Open-Reasoner-Zero: An Open Source Approach to Scaling Up Reinforcement Learning on the Base Model
papers.takeaway: Pushes open RL-on-base-model training for reasoning, useful for reproducibility-minded readers.

papers.title: Qwen3 Technical Report
papers.takeaway: A modern hybrid thinking/non-thinking model family report, useful for how reasoning modes enter frontier models.

papers.title: Skywork Open Reasoner 1 Technical Report
papers.takeaway: A recent open-reasoner report focused on long-CoT math and code reasoning.

papers.title: OpenThoughts: Data Recipes for Reasoning Models
papers.takeaway: A practical reference for open reasoning data construction, filtering, and recipe sharing.

papers.title: QwQ-32B: Embracing the Power of Reinforcement Learning
papers.takeaway: A widely discussed open reasoning model release that makes RL-centered reasoning visible to builders.

papers.title: Llama-Nemotron: Efficient Reasoning Models
papers.takeaway: A strong industrial open reasoning family, useful for comparing distillation and efficiency-focused recipes.

papers.title: AceReason-Nemotron: Advancing Math and Code Reasoning through SFT and RL Synergy
papers.takeaway: Highlights the interaction between supervised reasoning data and RL for math/code performance.

papers.title: A Survey on Test-Time Scaling in Large Language Models: What, How, Where, and How Well?
papers.takeaway: A convenient entry point for readers trying to organize search, self-refinement, verification, and budget control.

papers.title: The Art of Scaling Test-Time Compute for LLMs
papers.takeaway: Organizes practical decisions around when and how to spend extra inference compute.

papers.title: Scaling Test-Time Compute for LLM Agents
papers.takeaway: Extends test-time scaling from single prompts to agent loops, tool calls, and multi-step workflows.

papers.title: LiveCodeBench: Holistic and Contamination Free Evaluation of Large Language Models for Code
papers.takeaway: A high-traffic code reasoning benchmark designed to reduce contamination and track evolving coding ability.

papers.title: Humanity's Last Exam
papers.takeaway: A viral hard benchmark for frontier models, useful for tracking whether reasoning claims survive expert-level questions.

papers.title: BrowseComp: A Simple Yet Challenging Benchmark for Browsing Agents
papers.takeaway: Makes web-browsing and information-seeking reasoning a concrete evaluation target.

papers.title: Robust Reasoning Benchmark: Assessing LLMs' Mathematical Reasoning Capabilities Against Natural Input Perturbations
papers.takeaway: Moves beyond headline math accuracy by probing whether reasoning is stable under natural perturbations.

papers.title: Does More Inference-Time Compute Really Help Robust Reasoning?
papers.takeaway: A useful counterweight to hype: more thinking can be brittle, wasteful, or even harmful under some perturbations.

papers.title: Scaling Test-Time Compute Optimally in LLM Agentic Coding
papers.takeaway: A timely bridge between coding agents and inference-time compute allocation.

papers.title: Phi-4 Technical Report
papers.takeaway: A compact reasoning-oriented model report centered on synthetic data and STEM ability.
